text: Yes we can, to opportunity and prosperity. Yes we can heal this nation. Yes we can repair this world. Yes we can.
speech: New Hampshire Primary Concession / 'Yes We Can' speech
context: The soaring, rhythmic conclusion that became the campaign's defining refrain.

text: We are the ones we've been waiting for. We are the change that we seek.
context: Empowering supporters to see themselves as the agents of change.

text: The audacity of hope! In the end, that is God's greatest gift to us, the bedrock of this nation.
context: The speech that launched Obama onto the national stage.

text: There is not a liberal America and a conservative America — there is the United States of America. There is not a black America and a white America and Latino America and Asian America — there's the United States of America.
context: Rejecting divisive identity politics in favor of a shared national story.

text: We did not come to fear the future. We came here to shape it.
context: Call to confidence and agency in the face of economic crisis.

text: Hope is that thing inside us that insists, despite all the evidence to the contrary, that something better awaits us if we have the courage to reach for it and to work for it and to fight for it.
context: Defining hope not as naive optimism but as disciplined courage.

text: If there is anyone out there who still doubts that America is a place where all things are possible; who still wonders if the dream of our founders is alive in our time; who still questions our capacity to restore ourselves and to remake this nation — I have news for you tonight: Yes we can.
speech: Election Night Victory Speech (Grant Park, Chicago)
context: The triumphant affirmation on the night he was elected the first African American president.

text: This is our moment. This is our time — to put our people back to work and open doors of opportunity for our kids; to restore prosperity and promote the cause of peace; to reclaim the American Dream and reaffirm that fundamental truth — that out of many, we are one.
context: Framing the election as a generational opportunity.

text: I am the son of a black man from Kenya and a white woman from Kansas. I was raised with the help of a white grandfather who survived a Depression to serve in Patton's Army during World War II and a white grandmother who worked on a bomber assembly line at Fort Leavenworth while he was overseas.
context: Personal story used to bridge America's racial history during the campaign.

text: The cynics may say that we will never build a more perfect union. But the cynics are wrong. We have made progress. And we will continue to make progress.
context: Rejecting cynicism while acknowledging the difficult work of perfecting the union.

text: We are the story of a people who crossed oceans and settled a continent, who won independence and freed slaves, who gave women the vote and marched for civil rights and pushed against the boundaries of our prejudice.
context: Recounting the American story as one of ongoing expansion of freedom.

text: We remain a young nation, but in the words of Scripture, the time has come to set aside childish things.
context: Calling for maturity in facing national challenges.

text: Change will not come if we wait for some other person or some other time. We are the ones we've been waiting for. We are the change that we seek.
context: The full context of the 'We are the ones' line.

text: Our journey has never been one of short-cuts or settling for less. It has not been the path for the faint-hearted — for those who prefer leisure over work, or seek only the pleasures of riches and fame.
context: Placing the moment in the long American story of sacrifice and striving.

text: The time has come to reaffirm our enduring spirit; to choose our better history; to carry forward that precious gift, that noble idea, passed on from generation to generation: the God-given promise that all are equal, all are free, and all deserve a chance to pursue their full measure of happiness.
context: Opening of his first inaugural, grounding the moment in founding ideals.

text: Starting today, we must pick ourselves up, dust ourselves off, and begin again the work of remaking America.
context: Call to renewal in the midst of the Great Recession.

text: For we know that our patchwork heritage is a strength, not a weakness. We are a nation of Christians and Muslims, Jews and Hindus — and non-believers.
context: Celebrating America's religious and philosophical diversity.

text: What the cynics fail to understand is that the ground has shifted beneath them — that the stale political arguments that have consumed us for so long no longer apply.
context: Declaring a post-partisan opening (at least aspirationally).

text: The success of our economy has always depended not just on the size of our gross domestic product, but on the reach of our prosperity; on the ability to extend opportunity to every willing heart — not out of charity, but because it is the surest route to our common good.
context: Linking broad-based prosperity to the health of the republic.

text: We will not apologize for our way of life, nor will we waver in its defense, and for those who seek to advance their aims by inducing terror and slaughtering innocents, we say to you now that our spirit is stronger and cannot be broken; you cannot outlast us, and we will defeat you.
context: Clear message to terrorists and adversaries on his first day in office.
